description_long: This windmill features a tall, octagonal stone tower constructed from gray bricks, topped with a steep, shingled roof in a tan color. The front of the structure includes a prominent arched doorway with a stone frame, suggesting an entry point for maintenance or access. Extending from the upper section of the tower is a large, four-bladed rotor made of wood, with each blade featuring rectangular cutouts for reduced wind resistance. The windmill's overall design is reminiscent of traditional European rural windmills, with robust masonry and a functional, rustic aesthetic. The blades are mounted on a horizontal axis, projecting outward from the tower's face. The structure is substantial and appears fixed, suitable for placement in parks, plazas, or as a historical landmark within an urban environment.
description: A tall, stone-brick windmill with a shingled roof and large wooden blades, featuring an arched entryway and traditional rustic design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cross of the wooden blades and the arched doorway at the base of the stone tower.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the shingled roof and the thickness of the stone tower, with the blades seen edge-on.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the shingled roof and the full span of the wooden blades, with no visible entryway.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the arched doorway, the side of the tower, and the wooden blades from the side.
